---
title: "3d Industry Subset Regressions"
author: "Jonathan"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Regression

Now, I want to compare the effect in each industry. So, I will create a subset of the data for each industry and then rerun the same, simple, controlled regression as when I did nation-wide.

```{r}
#Setup code
library(dplyr)
regression4_variables = data_set %>%
  select(total_program_expenses, gdp_change_percent, year, state, 
         industry
         ) %>%
  mutate(
    log_program_expenses = log(total_program_expenses + 1)
  )
#Regression code
model4 = feols(log_program_expenses ~ gdp_change_percent + i(industry) | year + state, data = regression2_variables)

#Output code
summary(model4)
```
